name: National Auto Sales
description_short: Metro Atlanta used car dealership offering in-house financing for all credit types, with down payments from $599 and monthly Equifax reporting to help rebuild credit.
description_long:
National Auto Sales is a Metro Atlanta-based used car dealership with 30 years in business and over 25,000 customers served. Operating as a buy-here-pay-here style dealer, the company specializes in pre-inspected, Carfax-certified used vehicles across multiple segments — SUVs, sedans, economy cars, and luxury vehicles — with 150+ units in inventory. The company markets itself as the Atlanta area's superstore for quality affordable vehicles and touts a 90% customer satisfaction rate based on its online reviews.

The dealership provides in-house financing designed for buyers across all credit profiles, including those with bad or damaged credit. Down payments start at $599–$1,499 on most vehicles, which the company claims is approximately $1,000 below the national average for used car purchases. Loan terms run up to four years. Customers can submit an online pre-approval application in minutes. Carfax vehicle history reports are available on request, and the company states its inventory contains no vehicles with salvage titles, fire damage, odometer rollbacks, or other major reported issues.

What distinguishes National Auto Sales from many used dealerships is its monthly credit reporting to Equifax. Buyers who make on-time payments can actively rebuild their credit history through their vehicle purchase — a feature that matters significantly to the credit-damaged demographic the company targets. Gap coverage is sold as an add-on to cover any loan balance not paid out by insurance in the event of a total loss. Vehicle pre-inspections are performed before sale. The company also emphasizes shorter loan terms and lower down payment requirements compared to the broader market.

National Auto Sales is a regional auto dealer, not a financial institution or general-purpose lender. Financing here is tied entirely to vehicle purchases on their lot and cannot be used for any other consumer finance purpose. Interest rates and APR are not disclosed anywhere on the website, which is a meaningful transparency gap for consumers comparison-shopping financing costs. The physical dealership serves Metro Atlanta exclusively, making it irrelevant to out-of-area consumers. For Atlanta-area buyers with bad credit who need reliable transportation and want simultaneous credit rebuilding, this dealership is a practical option — but buyers should request full loan terms, compare effective APRs, and review the warranty terms before committing. Many lenders offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments over 12 to 60 months. For those with damaged credit, credit repair companies can dispute inaccurate items with all three bureaus. Secured credit cards and credit builder loans offer structured paths to rebuilding credit scores over time.
pros: Down payments as low as $599–$1,499 on most vehicles, advertised as ~$1,000 below the national average
Reports monthly to Equifax, allowing on-time payments to rebuild credit history
All vehicles are Carfax-certified with no salvage titles, fire damage, or odometer rollbacks
Accepts all credit types including bad credit — no minimum credit score mentioned
Online pre-approval application available — approval in minutes
4-year loan terms designed to keep monthly payments manageable
Gap coverage available as an add-on for total-loss accident protection
30 years in business with 25,000+ customers served
cons: No interest rates or APR disclosed anywhere on the website — impossible to compare financing costs without applying
Financing is tied to on-lot vehicle purchases only — not usable for any other consumer finance need
Metro Atlanta physical location only — not available to consumers outside the Atlanta area
Inventory of 150+ vehicles is relatively small, limiting selection compared to larger dealers or online platforms
No information on minimum loan amounts, income requirements, or residency restrictions
best_for: Metro Atlanta residents with bad or damaged credit who need a vehicle and have limited upfront cash
Credit-rebuilding consumers who want monthly Equifax reporting tied to an auto loan
Buyers who want a shorter loan term (up to 4 years) and lower down payment than traditional dealerships require
First-time buyers or those recovering from bankruptcy who cannot qualify at conventional auto lenders
diagnosis: National Auto Sales is best suited for Metro Atlanta consumers with bad or damaged credit who need a vehicle and want their payments reported to Equifax for credit rebuilding. The main caveat is that APR and full loan terms are not disclosed on the website, so buyers must apply and compare costs before committing — and the offering is limited to Atlanta-area residents purchasing from their physical lot.
services: Used vehicle sales — SUVs, sedans, economy, and luxury segments
Vehicle warranty (listed in menu, terms not disclosed)
